method_atom: A large-N effective field theory in which many flavors of gapless units couple strongly to a fluctuating collective/mediating field, and whose perturbative expansion is organized topologically: diagrams are drawn in a double-line representation and classified by the genus of the surface they can be embedded in, so that all planar contributions survive at leading order. Because abundant low-energy excitations keep the coupling strong even as the flavor number grows, the theory stays non-perturbative and an infinite class of corrections must be resummed to fix the leading behavior of the two-point (Green's) function, while the mediating field's self-energy is saturated at one loop.
object_atom: A patch of a two-dimensional metallic Fermi surface carrying N fermion flavors, coupled to a transverse U(1) gauge field in 2+1 dimensions — a non-Fermi-liquid electronic state.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the genus/planar-diagram organization of a strongly-coupled large-N theory that resists weak-coupling control; the domain-specific part is the 2+1D gauge-field-coupled Fermi surface, an unavoidably physics-tied object since the topological classification here is generated by low-energy modes near that surface.
